---
name: ddx
description: Operates the DDx toolkit for document-driven development. Covers beads (work items), the queue, executions, agents, harnesses, personas, reviews, spec-id. Use when the user says "do work", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "execute a bead", "review this", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", "check against spec", "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "what's blocking the queue", "create a bead", "file this as work", "run an agent", "dispatch", "use a persona", "how am I doing", "ddx doctor", or mentions any ddx CLI command.
---

# DDx

DDx (Document-Driven Development eXperience) is a CLI platform for
document-driven development. It ships a bead tracker (portable work
items with acceptance criteria), a task-execution boundary (DDx
forwards raw passthrough constraints for harness, provider, model,
and profile while power travels as `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds;
Fizeau owns concrete routing, provider/model discovery, aliases,
fuzzy matching, catalog lookups, and transcript/session rendering), a
persona system (bindable AI personalities), a library registry
(plugins with prompts, templates, personas), and git-aware
synchronization. This skill makes any skills-compatible coding agent
(Claude Code, OpenAI Codex, Gemini CLI, etc.) understand and operate
the DDx surface correctly.

## How this skill works

The skill body you're reading is an **overview** plus an **intent
router**. The real domain guidance lives in `reference/*.md` files.

**Directive: before responding to any DDx-related request, read the
matching reference file from the router table below. The router is
not optional — your answer must be grounded in the reference file's
guidance, not this overview alone.**

## Vocabulary

Single source of truth for DDx terms. Every reference file uses these
exact definitions.

- **Bead** — a portable work item (task, bug, epic, chore) with
  metadata, dependencies, and acceptance criteria. `ddx bead create`.
- **Queue** — the set of active beads. The *execution-ready queue* is
  `status=open` work whose dependencies are closed and which is not parked by
  cooldown, supersession, or execution-eligibility policy. `ddx bead ready`,
  `ddx bead status`.
- **Lifecycle status** — the persisted bead state: `proposed`, `open`,
  `in_progress`, `blocked`, `closed`, or `cancelled`.
- **Ready** — a bead whose dependencies are all closed and which is
  eligible to be picked up next. `ddx bead ready`.
- **Blocked** — `status=blocked` work paused by a hard external recheckable
  blocker. Ordinary dependency waits are derived queue state, not blocked
  status. `ddx bead blocked`.
- **Proposed** — work awaiting operator decision before autonomous execution.
  Proposed beads are not worker-eligible until accepted, rewritten, split,
  blocked externally, cancelled, or otherwise resolved.
- **Claim** — mark a bead as in-progress by an agent (concurrent-write
  protection). `ddx bead update <id> --claim`.
- **Close** — mark a bead as done, with evidence (session, commit
  SHA). `ddx bead close <id>`. Beads only close on execution outcomes
  `success` or `already_satisfied`.
- **Run** — one task invocation atom. `ddx run` calls Fizeau `Execute`
  once with prompt/config, `MinPower`/`MaxPower` bounds, and optional
  raw passthrough constraints.
- **Try** — one bead attempt in an isolated worktree. `ddx try <id>` wraps
  `ddx run` with bead prompt resolution, evidence capture, and merge/preserve
  finalization.
- **Work** — drain the bead queue. `ddx work` picks ready beads and invokes
  `ddx try`; it owns queue iteration and retry policy.
- **Worker** — a `ddx work` / `ddx try` process scoped to a specific project
  root. Do not call every `ddx work` process a "DDx worker" when multiple
  projects are visible. If the user says "DDx worker" without a qualifier,
  assume they mean the worker for the current/requested project. Say "worker
  for `<project>`" or "DDx-powered worker"; reserve "DDx repo worker" for work
  whose project root is the DDx repository.
- **Execution** — a generic DDx execution run (FEAT-010). Includes execution
  definitions, execution records, and execution evidence under `.ddx/runs/<id>/`.
- **Agent** — an AI coding agent (Claude, Codex, Gemini, etc.)
  invoked via a harness. Not a subagent (harness-specific — see
  below).
- **Harness** — a Fizeau routing concept. DDx may pass `--harness` as
  an operator-supplied constraint, but DDx does not validate, rank, fallback, or
  branch on harness names.
- **Persona** — a Markdown file (YAML frontmatter + body) that
  defines an AI personality. DDx injects the body as a system-prompt
  addendum to `ddx run`. `ddx persona list/show/bind`.
- **Role** — an abstract function (e.g., `code-reviewer`,
  `test-engineer`) a workflow can reference. Projects bind roles to
  personas.
- **Binding** — a project-specific `role: persona` map in
  `.ddx/config.yaml` under `persona_bindings`.
- **Power bounds** — `MinPower` and optional `MaxPower` integers passed to the
  upstream execution service. DDx may raise `MinPower` on eligible retries;
  Fizeau owns concrete route selection within those bounds.
- **Plugin** — a self-contained extension installed to
  `.ddx/plugins/<name>/`. The default `ddx` plugin (personas,
  prompts, patterns, templates) is auto-installed by `ddx init`.
  `ddx install <name>`.
- **Skill** — an agentskills.io-standard directory (SKILL.md +
  optional `reference/`, `evals/`, `scripts/`). This `ddx` skill is
  the one DDx ships. Plugins can ship additional skills.
- **Subagent** — a harness-local concept for running a prompt in an
  isolated context (Claude Code's `.claude/agents/` + `context:
  fork`; Codex's `agents/`; others differ). DDx does not specify subagent
  orchestration; that remains harness business.
- **Update** — refresh plugin/toolkit content to a newer version.
  `ddx update [<plugin>]`.
- **Upgrade** — replace the DDx binary with a newer release.
  `ddx upgrade`.
- **Review** — two distinct concepts. **Bead review**
  (`ddx bead review <id>`) grades a completed bead against its
  acceptance criteria. **Comparison/adversarial review** is a workflow skill
  composition over `ddx run`, not a core quorum flag. See `reference/review.md`.
- **Governing artifact** — the document that authorizes a bead's
  work: a FEAT-\*, SD-\*, TD-\*, or ADR-\* under `docs/`. Referenced
  via `spec-id`.
- **Spec-id** — the `spec-id: <ID>` custom field on a bead pointing
  at its governing artifact.

## Intent router

Before responding, read the matching file.

| User says / asks about | Read this file |
|---|---|
| "what should I work on next", "what's blocking the queue", "review with fresh eyes", "fold in this guidance", "review again", "break this down into specs and beads", "make this testable", broad queue orientation or planning | `reference/interactive.md` |
| write/plan work, "create a bead", "file this as work", bead metadata, acceptance criteria, dependencies | `reference/beads.md` |
| `ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", "drain the queue", "run the next bead", "start the worker", "start N workers and watch", "monitor the drain", verify-and-close | `reference/work.md` |
| "review this", "check against spec", bead review, quorum review, code review, adversarial check | `reference/review.md` |
| "assess bead readiness", "score a bead", "triage a failed attempt", "refine a bead", bead authoring lint | `bead-lifecycle/` |
| "run an agent", "dispatch", harness/provider/model passthrough, power, effort, "use a persona", role bindings | `reference/agents.md` |
| "what's on the queue", "what's ready", "how am I doing", health check, "ddx doctor", sync status | `reference/status.md` |

If the intent spans multiple files (e.g., "create a bead and then
run it"), read beads.md first, then work.md. If no match, ask the
user which concept they mean rather than guessing.

## Top-level policy reminders

These apply across all DDx operations. Do not restate them in every
reference file; do not violate them.

- **Never edit `.ddx/beads.jsonl` directly.** All tracker changes go
  through `ddx bead create/update/close/dep`. Direct edits corrupt
  bead history and cannot be audited.
- **Tracker changes are commit-worthy.** After `ddx bead create`,
  `update`, `dep add/remove`, or `close`, commit the resulting
  `.ddx/beads.jsonl` change — either as a tracker-only commit or
  folded into the same commit as related implementation changes.
- **Preserve bead-attempt commit history.** Branches containing
  `ddx try` / `ddx work` execution commits carry an audit trail. **Never
  squash, rebase, filter, or amend** these commits. Use only
  `git merge --ff-only` or `git merge --no-ff` when merging.
  `gh pr merge --squash` and `--rebase` are forbidden on these
  branches.
- **Work in worktrees for parallel agents.** Use `wt switch -c
  <branch>` (worktrunk) or equivalent to give each concurrent agent
  its own isolated checkout. `ddx try` does this automatically;
  manual parallel work should too.
- **Power-first execution dispatch.** Default to `ddx run`/`ddx try`/`ddx work`
  with power bounds. `--harness`, `--provider`, and `--model` are passthrough
  constraints only; DDx must not use them for routing policy.

## Links out

- Full CLI reference: `ddx --help`, `ddx <subcommand> --help`.
- Governing feature specs: see `FEAT-*` documents under your
  project's `docs/` tree — especially the CLI, beads, agent-service,
  executions, and skills features.
- Personas README: shipped by the default `ddx` plugin at
  `.ddx/plugins/ddx/personas/README.md`.
- Open standard this skill conforms to:
  [agentskills.io](https://agentskills.io).

data.output:
# Work — Drain the Queue, Execute, Verify, Close

## Mode note

This reference covers **`bead_execution` mode**: explicit worker commands such as
`ddx work`, `ddx try <id>`, "execute bead `<id>`", or "start the worker".

If the user said "what should I work on next?", "what's blocking the queue?", or
"work the queue" without an explicit bead ID or `ddx work` command, they are in
**`interactive-steward`** mode — see `reference/interactive.md`. The steward
plans; the worker executes. Do not instruct manual ready-bead implementation in
response to broad orientation prompts.

---

"Doing work" in DDx means draining the ready queue: pick the top ready bead,
run one or more `ddx try` attempts, verify the result, and close the bead on
success or leave it available for a future eligible retry.

## Default surface: `ddx work`

```bash
ddx work
```

`ddx work` drains the queue by picking ready beads and invoking `ddx try`.
`ddx try` wraps `ddx run`, which is the single agent invocation primitive. DDx
owns queue iteration, attempt evidence, and retry policy; Fizeau owns concrete
routing, provider/model discovery, alias resolution, fuzzy matching, catalog
lookups, and transcript/session rendering.

Flags worth knowing:

- `--once` — pick one bead and stop (don't loop).
- `--watch` — keep running after the current ready queue drains; wait for newly-ready beads.
- `--idle-interval <dur>` — sleep duration between empty-queue scans in watch mode (default 30s).
- `--min-power <n>` / `--max-power <n>` — requested agent power bounds.
- `--top-power` — choose a `MinPower` threshold from the service's reported
  power bands.
- `--harness <name>` / `--provider <name>` / `--model <ref>` / `--profile <name>` — passthrough constraints only. DDx sends them
  unchanged and does not route on them.

## Pre-claim Intake and Silent-Idle Diagnosis

The worker's pre-claim intake hook evaluates a `readiness_checks` payload before
claiming a bead. The canonical verdict forms are:

- JSON bool `true` -> `pass`
- JSON bool `false` -> `fail`
- JSON strings are passed through after trimming
- `null` or absent -> empty

`ClassifyReadinessWithMode`
(`cli/internal/agent/readiness_classification.go:56-115`) is the mapping layer
between readiness classifications and worker behavior:

- `system_unready` / `intake_error` are hard errors from the hook, but the
  worker fail-opens and skips the claim instead of parking the bead.
- `needs_refine` is warn-only in warn mode and becomes operator-attention /
  park in block mode.
- `operator_required` parks the bead.
- `needs_split` parks the bead for decomposition.

When the worker appears to idle on a full queue, inspect `loop.idle` events
first. That event stream is the diagnostic surface for a silent stall. Repeated
identical blocker details are escalated after
`preClaimIdleEscalationThreshold` cycles (`ddx-df77e668`), which should convert
the stall into a `loop.operator_attention` signal instead of letting the worker
spin forever. The main blocker codes to distinguish are
`preclaim_systemic` and `preclaim_tracker_contention`.

If the queue is still making claims but the rate is suspiciously low, use the
claim-success-rate warning knobs `--claim-rate-window` and
`--claim-rate-threshold` to surface that condition.

## Are workers running for this project?

When someone asks "is the worker still working beads?" or "how is the queue
progressing?", do not answer with a global `ps aux | grep -E 'ddx work|ddx try'`
scan. That returns workers from every checkout on the host and falsely
signals progress on whichever project happens to be asked about.

```bash
ddx work status                # live workers scoped to this project
ddx work status --json         # machine-readable
ddx work status --all-projects # explicit cross-project escape hatch
```

`ddx work status` defaults to the current project root (CLI flag → env
`DDX_PROJECT_ROOT` → CWD git root). It reports pid, age, command line,
project root, and — when inferable from argv or an isolated execute-bead
worktree — the active bead ID. Always name the project root the answer
applies to; never substitute a worker from a different repository as
evidence that this project is progressing.

When multiple repositories have workers, report them project-first:

```text
<project-root>
  pid=<pid> bead=<bead-id> phase=<phase>
```

Use "worker for `<project-root>`" for project-scoped status. Use
"DDx-powered worker" for a generic `ddx work` process. Use "DDx repo worker"
only when the project root is the DDx repository. If the user says "DDx
worker" without a qualifier, assume the current/requested project and do not
surface workers from other projects.

`--all-projects` is the only sanctioned way to ask "what ddx workers
exist anywhere on this host?" — use it only when the question is
explicitly cross-project.

## Primitive: `ddx try`

For targeted re-runs, debugging, or running a specific bead:

```bash
ddx try <bead-id>
ddx try <bead-id> --from <rev>      # base commit override
ddx try <bead-id> --no-merge        # preserve result, don't land
```

`ddx try` runs one bead attempt in an isolated git worktree. It calls `ddx run`
for the actual agent invocation.

## Pick by default, not by ID

Under normal operation, **don't specify a bead ID**. `ddx work` picks
the top ready bead based on priority + dependency satisfaction. Only
pin a specific ID when debugging (`ddx try <id>`) or
when the queue ordering would pick the wrong bead and you need to
override.

## Starting and monitoring workers in the background

When the user asks you to **start workers and watch progress**, do
not invent your own `tail | grep` pipeline — the obvious shapes have
all bitten in the field. Use these vetted patterns.

### One worker, foreground drain

If the user just wants to drain once and read the result at the end,
run `ddx work` directly and let it print. Don't wrap with `head`,
`tail`, or `tee` — pipes to those tools buffer stdout for the entire
run, so the user sees nothing during execution and everything at the
end.

### One or more workers, watch live

```bash
# Start each worker, one log per worker:
ddx work > /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>&1 &   # repeat for worker2, etc.

# Stream with per-worker prefixes (drops the `==>` separator noise
# that `tail -F` injects when switching files):
(tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker1.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w1: "$0; fflush()}' &
 tail -F /tmp/ddx-worker2.log 2>/dev/null | awk '{print "w2: "$0; fflush()}' &
 wait) | grep -E --line-buffered \
  "^w[0-9]+: (▶|✗|✓|→ |worker exited|attempts:|failed:|landing worktree has|lock_contention|panic|FATAL|excluded|caller_excluded|ladder exhausted|escalation|powerClass=|do route|i/o timeout|connection refused|provider error)"
```

Why those specific tokens — together they cover **every actionable
event** in a drain:

- `▶ <bead>` — a new bead was claimed. `✗` and `✓` close it.
- `→` — outcome arrow, also follows a bead-state transition.
- `worker exited` / `attempts:` / `failed:` — end-of-drain summary.
- `landing worktree has` — pre-claim hook hit a stale index; queue
  is jammed if recovery doesn't kick in.
- `lock_contention`, `panic`, `FATAL` — infrastructure problems.
- `excluded`, `caller_excluded`, `ladder exhausted`,
  `escalation`, `powerClass=` — the escalation path actually
  firing; absence on a failed bead means the loop is silently
  no-op'ing instead of climbing the ladder.
- `do route` — the implementation-phase routing decision, surfaces
  the provider/model that's about to be hit. Without this in the
  alternation, the monitor goes quiet during the most interesting
  window (provider call) and only resurfaces on success/failure.
- `i/o timeout`, `connection refused`, `provider error` — transport
  failures. Two in a row to the same `(provider, model)` is the
  signature of routing that isn't honoring exclusions.

### What NOT to do

- **Don't wrap `ddx work` in `| tail -N`** to "see the last N
  lines". The pipe buffers until the producer exits, so you see
  nothing for the whole run. Redirect to a file and tail the file
  separately.
- **Don't grep `^==>` into the alternation.** Without the awk
  prefix, `tail -F file1 file2` emits `==> filename <==` whenever
  the active file switches — every switch becomes a noise event in
  a Monitor pipeline. Strip those, or prefix lines with `awk`.
- **Don't tail without `--line-buffered`/`fflush()`.** Pipe
  buffering means events arrive in 4KB chunks (often minutes apart)
  instead of as they happen. The `awk` form above flushes per line.
- **Don't share one log file across workers.** Interleaved output
  destroys attributability and any chance of debugging which worker
  hit what.

## Verify independently before closing

**Agents hallucinate successful completions.** Do not trust the
agent's self-report. Before closing a bead:

1. Run the acceptance-criteria command yourself (from the bead's
   `accept:` field). If it's `go test ./foo/...`, run that.
2. Check the resulting commit against the in-scope file list from
   the bead description. Out-of-scope files touched? Reject the
   attempt.
3. Read the commit message — does it reference the bead ID?
   (`[ddx-<id>]` or similar in the commit subject is the convention.)

If all three pass: close the bead.

## Close on success, unclaim on failure

DDx try/work outcomes form a specific taxonomy. Each outcome
maps to a concrete follow-up action:

During a live `ddx work` / `ddx try` attempt, the current bead's lifecycle is
orchestrator-owned. The worker executing that bead must not run
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`,
`ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>` for the
current bead. The Step 0 split-first path still allows child-bead
`ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add`, and the parent-note update
`ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.

| Outcome | Meaning | Action |
|---|---|---|
| `success` | Tests pass, AC met, commit landed | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `already_satisfied` | No changes needed (AC was already green) | `ddx bead close <id>` |
| `no_changes` | Agent returned without producing commits and wrote `no_changes_rationale.txt` | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `no_evidence_produced` | Agent exited without a commit or rationale | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate harness/commit failure |
| `land_conflict` | Merge conflict on landing the result | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `post_run_check_failed` | Tests or gate failed after landing | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |
| `execution_failed` | Agent subprocess errored (timeout, crash, provider error) | Leave open, **unclaim** |
| `structural_validation_failed` | Result failed structural sanity check | Leave open, **unclaim**, investigate |

`ddx work` applies these actions automatically. If you're running
`ddx try` directly, apply them manually: `ddx bead update <id>
--unclaim` to release a bead after a non-closing outcome, so another
worker can pick it up.

**Never leave a bead half-owned** — every execution either closes or
unclaims.

## Testing expectations for bead implementations

When an agent implements a bead, its output should include tests
that exercise the new code:

- **Unit tests** for logic with in-memory stubs for first-party
  collaborators. Favor stubs over mocks; mocks that assert on call
  sequences test implementation, not behavior.
- **Integration tests** against real collaborators where the cost
  is small (real git in a temp dir, real DB in a temp file, real
  HTTP via a local test server).
- **Real e2e tests** at the outermost boundary. E2e tests that mock
  the database or the network are unit tests lying about their
  scope.

package server

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"sync/atomic"
	"syscall"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/testutils"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// newIdleHandle returns a manually-constructed workerHandle registered with m
// for tests that drive the watchdog directly without starting a real loop.
func newIdleHandle(t *testing.T, m *WorkerManager, id string, beadID string, startedAt, lastPhaseTS time.Time) (*workerHandle, *atomic.Bool) {
	t.Helper()
	var cancelled atomic.Bool
	cancel := context.CancelFunc(func() { cancelled.Store(true) })
	h := &workerHandle{
		record: WorkerRecord{
			ID:          id,
			Kind:        "work",
			State:       "running",
			Status:      "running",
			ProjectRoot: m.projectRoot,
			StartedAt:   startedAt,
			CurrentBead: beadID,
			CurrentAttempt: &CurrentAttemptInfo{
				AttemptID: id + "-a1",
				BeadID:    beadID,
				Phase:     "running",
				StartedAt: startedAt,
			},
		},
		cancel:       cancel,
		progressDone: make(chan struct{}),
		lastPhaseTS:  lastPhaseTS,
	}
	m.mu.Lock()
	m.workers[id] = h
	m.mu.Unlock()
	return h, &cancelled
}

// seedClaimedBead creates a ready bead and claims it so Unclaim() has work
// to do. Returns the bead store.
func seedClaimedBead(t *testing.T, root string, beadID string) *bead.Store {
	t.Helper()
	ddx := testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, root)
	store := bead.NewStore(ddx)
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:        beadID,
		Title:     "watchdog test bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, store.Claim(beadID, "worker-test"))
	return store
}

// TestWatchdogSweepReapsStalledWorker is the core AC test: a worker whose
// runtime exceeds WatchdogDeadline AND whose current attempt has sat without
// a phase transition longer than StallDeadline is reaped by one sweep.
func TestWatchdogSweepReapsStalledWorker(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-wd-01")

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	m.WatchdogDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.StallDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.WatchdogKillGrace = 10 * time.Millisecond
	defer m.StopWatchdog()

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	// StartedAt is 1s in the past and lastPhaseTS is 1s in the past —
	// both exceed the 1ms deadlines.
	h, cancelled := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-wd-01", "ddx-wd-01",
		now.Add(-1*time.Second), now.Add(-1*time.Second))

	m.watchdogSweep(now)

	m.mu.Lock()
	reaped := h.reaped
	state := h.record.State
	reapReason := h.record.ReapReason
	m.mu.Unlock()

	assert.True(t, reaped, "handle should be flagged reaped")
	assert.Equal(t, "reaped", state, "record.State must flip to 'reaped'")
	assert.Equal(t, "watchdog", reapReason)
	assert.True(t, cancelled.Load(), "watchdog must invoke cancel() for in-process workers")

	// AC: bead claim must be released back to open.
	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-wd-01")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status,
		"bead must return to open after watchdog releases the claim")

	// AC: a bead.reaped event must be on the tracker with reason=watchdog
	// and a duration mentioned in the body.
	events, err := store.EventsByKind("ddx-wd-01", "bead.reaped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1, "expected exactly one bead.reaped event")
	assert.Equal(t, "watchdog", events[0].Summary)
	assert.Contains(t, events[0].Body, "runtime=")
	assert.Contains(t, events[0].Body, "stalled=")
	assert.Contains(t, events[0].Body, "reason=watchdog")
}

// TestWatchdogSweepSkipsHealthyWorker: a worker whose runtime is under
// WatchdogDeadline (or whose lastPhaseTS is fresh) must NOT be reaped.
func TestWatchdogSweepSkipsHealthyWorker(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-wd-healthy")

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	m.WatchdogDeadline = 1 * time.Hour // far in the future
	m.StallDeadline = 1 * time.Hour
	defer m.StopWatchdog()

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	h, cancelled := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-wd-healthy", "ddx-wd-healthy",
		now.Add(-1*time.Second), now.Add(-1*time.Second))

	m.watchdogSweep(now)

	m.mu.Lock()
	reaped := h.reaped
	state := h.record.State
	m.mu.Unlock()

	assert.False(t, reaped, "healthy worker must not be flagged reaped")
	assert.Equal(t, "running", state)
	assert.False(t, cancelled.Load(), "cancel() must not fire on healthy worker")
}

// TestWatchdogSweepSkipsNoCurrentAttempt: a worker between beads (CurrentAttempt nil)
// has no phase to stall on; do not reap even if past WatchdogDeadline.
func TestWatchdogSweepSkipsNoCurrentAttempt(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	m.WatchdogDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.StallDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	defer m.StopWatchdog()

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	h, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-idle-ok", "", now.Add(-1*time.Second), now.Add(-1*time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	h.record.CurrentAttempt = nil
	h.record.CurrentBead = ""
	m.mu.Unlock()

	m.watchdogSweep(now)

	m.mu.Lock()
	reaped := h.reaped
	m.mu.Unlock()
	assert.False(t, reaped, "idle worker with no current attempt must not be reaped")
}

// TestWatchdogSweepReapIsIdempotent: a second sweep over an already-reaped
// handle must not re-fire cancel() or emit a second bead.reaped event.
func TestWatchdogSweepReapIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-wd-idem")

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	m.WatchdogDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.StallDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.WatchdogKillGrace = 1 * time.Millisecond
	defer m.StopWatchdog()

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	_, _ = newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-idem", "ddx-wd-idem",
		now.Add(-1*time.Second), now.Add(-1*time.Second))

	m.watchdogSweep(now)
	m.watchdogSweep(now) // second sweep must be a no-op

	events, err := store.EventsByKind("ddx-wd-idem", "bead.reaped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Len(t, events, 1, "second sweep must not double-emit bead.reaped")
}

// TestWatchdogDeadlinesConfigurable verifies that ddx server config values
// flow into the manager via LoadWithWorkingDir.
func TestWatchdogDeadlinesConfigurable(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	ddx := filepath.Join(root, ddxroot.DirName)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(ddx, 0o755))
	// Minimal config.yaml with a server.watchdog_deadline / stall_deadline override.
	cfgPath := filepath.Join(ddx, "config.yaml")
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(cfgPath, []byte(
		"version: \"1.0\"\n"+
			"library:\n  path: .ddx/plugins/ddx\n  repository:\n    url: https://example/lib\n    branch: main\n"+
			"server:\n"+
			"  watchdog_deadline: 42m\n"+
			"  stall_deadline: 7m\n",
	), 0o644))

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	assert.Equal(t, 42*time.Minute, m.WatchdogDeadline,
		"WatchdogDeadline must come from config.server.watchdog_deadline")
	assert.Equal(t, 7*time.Minute, m.StallDeadline,
		"StallDeadline must come from config.server.stall_deadline")

	// Defaults are applied for unset fields.
	w, s, c, g := m.watchdogDeadlines()
	assert.Equal(t, 42*time.Minute, w)
	assert.Equal(t, 7*time.Minute, s)

.ddx/beads.jsonl:26:{"created_at":"2026-06-27T22:49:40.744689961Z","description":"PROBLEM
Server-managed workers still start through the in-process goroutine path, so the server owns only a cancelable context and cannot prove it owns a killable worker process group. A managed worker also needs its process-group identity persisted in status.json, not just memory, so restart/shutdown can reason about the same worker after a process exit.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-462 starts a worker by building a record, creating a context, and launching runWorker in goroutines; there is no OS process boundary in that path.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:88-135 defines WorkerRecord with PID and PIDAlive, but no persisted PGID/process-group field.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1711-1720 writes status.json from the record as-is, so any managed process ownership must be recorded on WorkerRecord to survive a restart.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a server-managed launch path that starts ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group instead of only launching runWorker in-process.
- Extend WorkerRecord and the persisted status.json shape to include the managed process-group identity needed for cleanup.
- Wire the server-owned work start flow through that managed launch path and keep the existing in-process path only as a test seam if needed.
- Add TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup to prove the managed worker record and status.json both show nonzero process-group ownership.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement stop, watchdog reap, or shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or the UI.
- Do not alter bead claim semantics beyond what the managed start path needs for its own record.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup proves a server-managed drain worker has nonzero PID/PGID in WorkerRecord and status.json.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerRecordsProcessGroup" passes.

.ddx/beads.jsonl:32:{"created_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:29.541930292Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-096129b9","depends_on_id":"ddx-a0a13392","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:50Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
The watchdog reaper still mirrors the Stop path's pid-limited cleanup. A stalled managed worker can be marked reaped and have its bead claim released, yet still leave descendants behind unless reap uses the same managed-tree cleanup path as Stop.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1347-1414 selects reaping candidates and passes only pid into reapWorker.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1417-1482 emits bead.reaped, releases the bead claim, and then calls terminateProcessGroup(pid, grace) only when pid > 0.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 shows the same pid-only limitation that exists in Stop, but the reap path is separate and can drift if it does not reuse the shared cleanup helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Route watchdog reaping through the same managed-worker tree cleanup helper used by Stop.
- Keep bead.reaped emission, claim release, and terminal-state persistence intact.
- Make the reap path idempotent so repeated sweeps do not double-clean the same worker.
- Add a process-tree test that proves watchdog reap follows the SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL path and releases the claimed bead.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not add restart policy logic in this bead.
- Do not change Stop beyond the shared helper reuse already required by the stop bead.
- Do not touch external workers or CLI commands.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree proves watchdog reap follows the same SIGTERM-to-SIGKILL cleanup path and releases the claimed bead.
2. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerWatchdogReapKillsProcessTree" passes.
3. make install is run after the code change lands.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","id":"ddx-096129b9","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:server","area:agent","area:workers","kind:feature","kind:reliability","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-24a3da84","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"open","title":"server: reap managed worker process trees through watchdog","updated_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:50.440483728Z"}

In a linked DDx worktree, `ddx bead show/export` reported ddx-d94f1857 as closed while `ddx work plan --json` still ranked the same bead as open and next claim. That can make a worker re-run already-completed work and breaks the queue as source of truth.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:286-320 resolves bead commands through beadWorkspaceRoot(), which calls git.FindNearestDDxWorkspace and can prefer a registered/master worktree. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:80-84 bypasses that path and opens bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot)), which prefers the local in-tree .ddx if present. In dogfood on 2026-06-13, the global worktree registry master pointed at an old execute-bead temp path, so bead commands and work plan observed different physical stores.

PROPOSED FIX
- Centralize queue store resolution for all bead/work/status commands behind one project-aware helper.
- Add a linked-worktree regression fixture where the local .ddx snapshot disagrees with the registered/master store and prove bead show, bead list, bead export, bead ready, work plan, and work status use the same store.
- Add a diagnostic when the worktree registry master points at a missing execute-bead path or a path outside the current repository family.
- Ensure the chosen store is visible in verbose/debug output so operators can identify split-brain state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl as the fix. Do not remove convention-root support. Do not change bead lifecycle semantics beyond consistent store selection.","events_attachment":"ddx-0be85773/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-0be85773","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:work","area:beads","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":10,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-149c0cdd","spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"work: unify bead store resolution across queue commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T20:37:30.719290151Z"}

Server-managed drain workers are still started as in-process goroutines, so their WorkerRecord usually has PID zero and no PGID. The server cannot reliably target the ddx work process tree that later launches claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:123-127 records only PID and has no persisted PGID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as managing in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:461-462 starts drainProgress and runWorker goroutines instead of a server-owned OS process. cli/internal/server/workers.go:414-436 writes the initial WorkerRecord before any managed worker process boundary exists.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a server-managed subprocess launch path for StartExecuteLoop in cli/internal/server/workers.go:361-464. Persist the ExecuteLoopWorkerSpec as today, then start the current ddx binary as ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> or an equivalent hidden internal entry point in its own process group. Record nonzero PID and PGID on WorkerRecord and status.json. Keep the existing in-process path only where tests explicitly inject it, and do not make externally reported workers look server-owned.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

A server-managed worker must be fully cleanable. Stop, watchdog reap, restart, and server shutdown must not leave ddx work, claude, codex, shell, or sleep descendants behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 says WorkerManager manages in-process work workers as goroutines. cli/internal/server/workers.go:438 creates a cancelable context, but no OS process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID and :1282-1286 terminates a process group only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 has the same limitation in watchdog reap. Because server-owned workers are often goroutines with PID zero, the server cannot guarantee child harness cleanup.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce a server-managed process boundary. Preferred implementation: spawn a child ddx work --server-managed <worker-id> in its own process group and record PID/PGID in WorkerRecord. If a transitional in-process path remains for tests, every harness child process group must be registered on the worker handle and killed through the same cleanup helper. Stop/reap/shutdown should send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, SIGKILL remaining descendants, release bead claims, persist terminal status, and be idempotent.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:64-68` makes production route/service callers fall back to `NewServiceFromWorkDir`. `cli/internal/agent/serviceconfig.go:85-97` documents that `NewServiceFromWorkDir` constructs a Fizeau service owning provider discovery and background quota/aliveness probes, with default `context.Background`. `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:483-490` and `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:531-536` call `agent.ResolveServiceFromWorkDir(projectRoot)` during worker route/power/exclusion preflight even for an explicit harness/model worker. - Constrain discovery/probe initialization to the requested harness/provider/model when the operator pins them, so unrelated provider CLIs are not launched.
- Ensure any discovery/probe subprocesses are descendants of a cancellable attempt/worker context and are reaped before the worker exits.
- Add route/preflight evidence that distinguishes selected execution provider from background discovery candidates.

NON-SCOPE

DDx/FIZ worker failures can still surface as underspecified execution failures when provider availability, model availability, credentials, endpoint reachability, or harness configuration differs across machines. DDx is the abstraction that should let unattended workers continue through provider variance; a generic provider failure that gives no typed cause, no fallback action, and no durable route-health evidence is a product bug.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:287-290 returns pre-dispatch Fizeau Execute errors as a generic `agent: execute: %w`, leaving the execute loop without a typed provider/model/configuration failure contract.
cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:332-376 copies final service error text into Result.Error/ExitCode, but downstream classification still depends on free-text heuristics rather than a required typed route-health outcome for provider unavailable, model unavailable, auth missing, quota/rate limit, endpoint timeout, or harness misconfiguration.
cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:516-544 and :588-612 contain the unpinned route-exclusion/escalation path, but that path only works when the failure is already recognized as provider_connectivity/no_viable_provider; untyped provider/configuration failures can stop or preserve the bead without proving fallback was attempted or intentionally impossible.

PROPOSED FIX
- Define a typed provider failure taxonomy at the DDx/Fizeau boundary: provider_connectivity, provider_auth, provider_rate_limit, provider_quota, provider_model_unavailable, provider_harness_unavailable, provider_config_invalid, no_viable_provider, and unknown_provider_failure.
- Normalize both pre-dispatch Execute errors and final failed events into that taxonomy before ExecuteBeadReport construction.
- For unpinned workers, convert retryable provider/model/harness availability failures into route-health evidence and continue fallback/escalation without parking the bead.
- For explicit operator pins, fail loudly with a typed hard-pin exhausted/operator-action report that names the pin and available remediation, without silently widening pins.
- Persist durable evidence on the bead/run showing requested constraints, resolved route if any, typed failure, retryability, whether fallback was attempted, and why fallback stopped.

NON-SCOPE
Do not move concrete route selection into DDx; Fizeau still owns routing, aliases, model discovery, and provider catalog behavior. Do not remove explicit operator pins. Do not call real providers in tests.","events_attachment":"ddx-3b721804/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-3b721804","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:agent","area:work","area:routing","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"-120","schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-9a2b4976","spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"agent: type provider failures and enforce fallback semantics","updated_at":"2026-06-13T18:38:23.850216294Z"}

The watchdog reap path must clean the same server-owned worker process tree as operator Stop and must release any claimed bead when a worker stalls. Today a stalled managed worker with PID zero can leave harness descendants behind.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1400-1408 records only rec.PID when selecting watchdog reap candidates. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1434-1450 emits bead.reaped and releases the bead claim before cleanup, which is correct and must be preserved. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1453-1457 then calls terminateProcessGroup only when pid > 0, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:1459-1462 falls back to context cancellation, which is insufficient for orphaned child harnesses.

PROPOSED FIX
Wire reapWorker through the same managed process-tree cleanup helper used by Stop. Preserve the ordering that appends bead.reaped and Unclaim before process termination, then send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, SIGKILL survivors, persist terminal reaped status, and keep reaping idempotent for a worker already marked reaped.

NON-SCOPE

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1885-1903 tells execute-bead agents that Step 0 may run `ddx bead create` for decomposition. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1981-1984 reiterates that Step 0 allows bead creation while current-bead lifecycle stays orchestrator-owned. The prompt requires `parent=<parent-id>`, but DDx does not enforce that an execute-bead-created child has the current bead as parent, inherits required labels/spec-id, or writes a valid `no_changes_rationale.txt` before mutating the real landing tracker. The dogfood attempt created top-level sample fixtures instead.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add a guarded decomposition command/path for execute-bead Step 0, or validate all bead-create mutations made from an execution worktree before they reach the landing tracker.
- Reject or quarantine execute-bead-created beads whose title/description/AC look like sample fixtures, whose parent is empty or not the current bead, or whose required inherited labels/spec-id are absent.
- Add cleanup/recovery that parks or cancels invalid child beads automatically and records an operator_attention event naming the invalid IDs.
- Ensure stale run-state from a killed worker does not keep `ddx work status` reporting active work after the owning worker process is gone.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove legitimate Step 0 decomposition. Do not rewrite or delete tracker history manually. Do not change normal operator-invoked `ddx bead create` behavior outside execute-bead/work execution contexts.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-aef128f7: the same aborted run also proved process-tree cleanup failures.

During ddx-42124c23 verification on 2026-06-13, `lefthook run pre-commit` exited 0 even though its `go-test` step printed FAIL for `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/cmd`. That makes the required automated gate a false green and lets broken verification pass.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
lefthook.yml:206-214 builds a package list from staged paths and runs `go test -short -race -timeout 30m "./$pkg"` inside a shell `for pkg in $packages; do ... done` loop. The script does not accumulate failures or `set -e`, so when an early package fails and a later package passes, the shell returns the last command status. In the observed run, `cmd` failed but `internal/activework` and `internal/agent` passed, and Lefthook reported a successful hook.

PROPOSED FIX
Make the staged-package go-test hook fail if any package test fails. Preserve per-package output. Add a deterministic test or script-level check that simulates two packages where the first fails and the second passes and proves the hook exits non-zero.

NON-SCOPE
Do not fix the unrelated existing `cmd` package failures in this bead unless they are needed to prove the hook status propagation. During ddx-0be85773 dogfood on 2026-06-13, `go test ./cmd/...` spawned real provider CLI children and repeatedly read `/home/erik/.cache/fizeau/discovery/*-subscription.refreshing`. There is no cmd-test-wide guard that forces Fizeau discovery/cache paths into a temp root or blocks real provider CLI lookup, so discovery falls through to the operator's real cache and live auth paths. Tests must be hermetic.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/testutils_test.go and cli/cmd/run_test_helpers_test.go set some hermetic env but do NOT redirect the Fizeau discovery/cache directories into the per-test temp root, and do NOT rewrite PATH so codex/claude/gemini resolve to fake binaries. A pinned DDx worker still launched a non-pinned provider child during the 2026-06-13 dogfood run. The worker command was `ddx work --watch --idle-interval 30s --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini --review-harness codex --review-model gpt-5.4-mini --request-timeout 20m` for bead ddx-aef128f7. At 2026-06-13T16:10:51Z, worker pid 4105977 still owned `codex exec ... -m gpt-5.4-mini` pid 4116435, but also owned `/home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/bin/gemini` pid 4130588. That violates the DDx/Fizeau provider-independence contract: explicit passthrough constraints must apply to every DDx-owned lifecycle, review, and execution subprocess, and DDx must not silently widen to a different harness/provider.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/agent/dispatch.go:70-82 and cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:208-224 preserve rcfg harness/provider/model only at the AgentRunRuntime dispatch boundary. cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:1456-1459 records route evidence after the provider returns, but there is no worker-level invariant or test that all DDx-owned child processes created during a pinned worker run match the requested harness/model or have explicit lifecycle routing evidence. Existing hidden lifecycle callers still use ClearRoutingPins in cli/internal/agent/recovery_reframe.go:89, cli/internal/agent/triage_hook.go:118, cli/internal/agent/lint_hook.go:132, and cli/internal/agent/preclaim_intake_hook.go:556; any one of these can create a provider child that escapes the operator pin.

PROPOSED FIX
- Add an execution-level regression that starts a pinned worker/attempt with fake codex and fake gemini/claude executables on PATH and fails if any DDx-owned child process invokes an unrequested provider binary.
- Audit every AgentRunRuntime with ClearRoutingPins=true in lifecycle/review/intake/reframe paths and make each caller either preserve explicit worker passthrough constraints or record an explicit, operator-approved autonomous route decision.
- Add worker process-tree attribution/evidence for provider child commands during lifecycle and execution phases so future failures name pid, phase, requested route, selected route, and command.
- Ensure the fix does not implement a DDx model catalog or provider fallback policy; DDx should pass constraints and enforce invariants, while Fizeau owns concrete route resolution.

NON-SCOPE
Do not remove support for Gemini, Claude, or other harnesses. Do not kill unrelated interactive provider sessions. Do not make DDx choose replacement models. Request-timeout process cleanup is covered by sibling bead ddx-aef128f7; this bead is specifically about preventing non-pinned provider children from launching in the first place.

INTERSECTIONS
- ddx-aef128f7: same dogfood run also proved request-timeout/process-tree cleanup failure; this bead supplies the provider-escape invariant that cleanup alone cannot solve.
- ddx-34d96048 and ddx-3d80038f: prior fixes covered pinned route preflight and decomposition routing, but this dogfood run shows another provider escape remains in execution/lifecycle subprocess ownership.","id":"ddx-6d9a8bdb","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:work","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers","provider-independence"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"-100","schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"work: prevent pinned workers from launching unrequested provider children","triage.estimated_difficulty":"hard","updated_at":"2026-06-13T16:36:47.040131857Z"}

During the 2026-06-13 dogfood merge recovery, `.ddx/beads.jsonl` had real Git conflict stages visible via `git ls-files -u` and `git show :1:.ddx/beads.jsonl`, but `ddx bead merge` failed with `read Git stage 1 for .ddx/beads.jsonl: exit status 128`. That forced abandoning the worktree instead of using DDx's supported tracker merge surface.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go registers `newBeadMergeCommand`, and the merge implementation reads Git conflict stages for `.ddx/beads.jsonl`. The observed error means the helper's stage-read command is not equivalent to the working `git show :1:.ddx/beads.jsonl` path in a linked worktree or conflict state. This violates AGENTS.md's rule to prefer supported `ddx bead` operations over direct tracker edits.

PROPOSED FIX
- Reproduce the conflict with a test repository that has stages 1/2/3 for `.ddx/beads.jsonl` in a linked worktree.
- Fix the Git stage reader to use a working-tree-root-relative path and the correct repository/worktree directory.
- Add verbose diagnostics that print the exact stage/path being read when stage access fails.
- Keep the merge command tracker-only; do not introduce ad hoc JSONL editing as recovery.

NON-SCOPE
Do not change bead merge semantics for non-conflicted files. Do not bypass Git conflict stages by reading arbitrary temporary files. Do not rewrite existing execute-bead history.","events_attachment":"ddx-81e3adcd/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-81e3adcd","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:beads","area:git","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:high","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":17,"schema_version":1,"session_id":"eb-30cb8b84","spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"beads: repair merge conflict stage reader","updated_at":"2026-06-13T21:00:37.533050054Z"}

.ddx/beads.jsonl:412:{"acceptance":"1. All child beads under this epic are closed or explicitly cancelled with rationale.

Operators currently keep DDx queue-drain workers alive with tmux windows, shell jobs, or direct ddx work processes. That makes it hard to monitor, restart, stop, reconcile, and clean worker process trees across helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as an in-process goroutine manager. cli/internal/server/workers.go:361 starts server-owned workers, but desired worker count and restart policy are not durable. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214 can only kill a process group when WorkerRecord.PID is set, which is often zero for goroutine workers. cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:1931 and :3271 expose start/stop, not durable desired-state supervision.

PROPOSED FIX
Implement docs/helix/06-iterate/server-managed-workers-plan-2026-06-13.md as a sequence of child beads: desired-state reconciliation, managed process cleanup, API/CLI/UI controls, Docker no-leak verification, review timeout supervision, existing worker-test regression repair, durable audit ignored-path repair, and dogfood migration.

NON-SCOPE
Do not make bead claiming server-authoritative. Do not adopt arbitrary already-running external ddx work processes as fully managed. .ddx/beads.jsonl:413:{"created_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:16.111482848Z","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ddx-a0a13392","depends_on_id":"ddx-071e9bd6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:47Z"}],"description":"PROBLEM
Stop currently performs ad hoc cleanup inside WorkerManager.Stop, releasing the claimed bead and then killing only the recorded pid when pid > 0. That path does not prove that a managed worker tree with fake claude/codex descendants is fully terminated, and the cleanup logic is not centralized for reuse by other lifecycle paths.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1312 contains Stop, including the current pid-gated terminateProcessGroup call.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234-1286 computes pid and only escalates when pid > 0, so cleanup is limited to the recorded leader process.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1298-1312 finalizes the worker after the ad hoc kill path, which makes idempotence depend on repeated copies of the same logic rather than a shared helper.

PROPOSED FIX
- Introduce a shared managed-worker tree cleanup helper that sends SIGTERM to the managed process group, waits WatchdogKillGrace, then SIGKILLs any remaining descendants.
- Make Stop call that helper for server-managed workers before terminalizing the record.
- Preserve the bead.stopped event, claim release, and terminal status writes so repeated Stop calls remain idempotent.
- Add fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children so the test can prove the full descendant tree is gone after Stop.
- Add a second Stop call assertion so the test proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change watchdog reaping in this bead.
- Do not implement server shutdown cleanup in this bead.
- Do not change external reported workers or interactive sessions.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, then proves Stop leaves none of those descendants running.
2. TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent calls Stop twice and proves no duplicate bead transition and no unrelated process kill.
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... -run "TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants|TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent" passes.
4. make install is run after the code change lands.
5. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","id":"ddx-a0a13392","issue_type":"task","labels":["area:server","area:agent","area:workers","kind:feature","kind:reliability","phase:build","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-24a3da84","priority":0,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"open","title":"server: stop managed worker trees with shared cleanup helper","updated_at":"2026-06-27T22:50:47.561451269Z"}

During the 2026-06-13 dogfood run for ddx-d94f1857, `ddx work --request-timeout 20m` did not bound the wall-clock provider attempt. The worker exceeded the timeout, then SIGINT printed "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully" but left descendant work active long enough that the operator had to manually kill the worker/test process group.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work.go wires request timeout into the work/try path, but the observed execution path through cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go and cli/internal/agent/service_run.go did not ensure the timeout context owned every subprocess descendant. The cleanup contract in docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md P8 requires DDx-owned execution resources and subprocesses to be reclaimed; the observed attempt violated that contract.

PROPOSED FIX
- Audit the work/try context propagation from `--request-timeout` through execute-bead and service-run so the deadline cancels the provider process group and any post-run test child.
- Add process-tree cleanup that targets only descendants owned by the attempt/worktree and records cleanup evidence.
- Make graceful shutdown wait for bounded cleanup and then hard-kill remaining attempt descendants before returning.
- Surface a durable operator-attention event if cleanup had to escalate from graceful cancellation to kill.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill unrelated interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not rely on tmux/session cleanup. Do not remove provider-specific harness support.","id":"ddx-aef128f7","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:work","area:agent","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":"3","schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"closed","title":"work: enforce request-timeout process-tree cleanup","updated_at":"2026-06-13T17:33:29.011856495Z"}

Operator Stop must fully clean a server-managed worker, including claude, codex, shell, and sleep descendants, without killing unrelated processes or duplicating bead transitions when Stop is called more than once.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1227 has an idempotence guard for in-memory handles, but the cleanup path still depends on a single recorded PID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 calls terminateProcessGroup only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1288-1289 then cancels the goroutine, which cannot guarantee cleanup of harness descendants when the server-owned worker lacks a process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:22-32 documents that callers must register only processes spawned in their own process group.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce or reuse one cleanup helper that terminates only server-owned managed worker process groups by PGID when available, falling back to registered child process groups for any transitional in-process harness path. Wire WorkerManager.Stop through that helper after the existing bead.stopped event and Unclaim call. The helper must send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, send SIGKILL to remaining descendants, report what it killed, and make repeated Stop calls no-ops for bead transitions and process cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

Server.Shutdown only closes the bead hub and stops land coordinators. It does not stop the worker manager or sweep managed worker process trees, so server-owned descendants can outlive the server process when the server shuts down.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/server.go:269-283 has no worker-manager shutdown call at all; it only closes the bead hub and coordinator registry.
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1323-1327 only stops the watchdog goroutine, so it does not clean live workers.

- Call that method from Server.Shutdown before closing the hub and coordinators.
- Keep the operation idempotent so repeated shutdown calls do not double-transition beads or kill unrelated processes.
- Add TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees as the shutdown proof.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not change Stop/Reap semantics beyond reusing the shared helper.
- Do not implement UI controls or GraphQL mutations in this bead.
- Do not adopt external workers.

ACCEPTANCE
1. TestWorkerManagerShutdownStopsManagedProcessTrees proves server/manager shutdown cleans all server-owned worker process groups.

A worker can finish a bead and then fail durable audit commit because .ddx/metrics or .ddx/attachments are ignored by the project. That leaves successful worker output requiring manual git add -f cleanup, which is incompatible with unattended server-managed draining.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
During dogfood on pqueue, a worker closed pqueue-6381a300 but exited operator attention with 'failed to commit durable audit outputs' because git add refused ignored .ddx/metrics/.ddx/attachments paths. cli/internal/agent/durable_audit.go:93 commitDurableAuditOutputsLocked scopes to trackerpaths.ManagedPathspecs, but :112-116 stages dirty paths using git add -A -- without -f. DDx-managed audit paths may be intentionally ignored by projects, but they still must be force-stageable when DDx commits durable audit output.

PROPOSED FIX
Change durable audit staging so DDx-managed tracker/audit pathspecs are staged even when ignored, likely by using git add -f -A for managed audit paths or a narrower force-add path that preserves ordinary ignored-file safety. Add regression coverage with a fixture repo that ignores .ddx/metrics and .ddx/attachments but still commits managed durable audit output.

NON-SCOPE
Do not globally force-add arbitrary ignored files. Do not change tracker storage formats. DDx work leaked a provider-specific routing requirement during dogfood: ddx work --harness codex --profile smart reached readiness with 'under-specified routing for harness="codex": supply --model, --policy, or --min-power', then continued into an execution path that launched codex exec without a model and produced no useful work. DDx/Fizeau is the surface that should handle provider availability and configuration; harness-only work requests must either resolve a viable route or fail before claim with durable actionable evidence.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/work.go:79 defines --harness and --model as opaque passthrough constraints, and cli/cmd/work.go:113 leaves --min-power defaulting to 0. cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:244-264 builds agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest with Model, Policy, Harness, MinPower, and MaxPower, but a harness-only work request can still reach Fizeau with Model empty and MinPower 0. The comment at cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:270-272 claims Harness+empty Model routes within eligible models using Profile/MinPower, but github.com/easel/fizeau v0.14.50 rejects subprocess harness empty-model routing with no policy/min-power at service_execute.go:202-207. In dogfood this surfaced as readiness warning plus an opaque codex exec child rather than an operator-visible routing failure or automatic viable fallback.

PROPOSED FIX
Make ddx work/try execution robust for harness-only requests. Either translate profile into the Fizeau policy field that satisfies routing, apply a configured/default min-power for auto-routable harnesses, or reject before claim with a durable routing/operator-attention event when no viable route can be formed. The work loop must not continue into an execution attempt after an under-specified routing preflight for the selected harness. Add tests that simulate Fizeau's under-specified error and prove DDx chooses/fails correctly without spawning an unmodeled provider process.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hard-code Codex-only model names as the fix. Do not remove explicit model pins. Do not require every project to configure every provider manually. Operators need to say 'project X should have N queue-drain workers' and have DDx keep that true across server ticks and restarts. Today the server can start a worker once, but the desired count and restart policy are not durable.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:192 defines WorkerManager as an in-memory goroutine manager with workers map state. cli/internal/server/workers.go:361 StartExecuteLoop writes per-worker spec/status, but there is no project-level desired-state file such as .ddx/workers/desired.json. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1971 ReconcileStaleWorkers handles stale records, not desired count, restart policy, or backoff.

PROPOSED FIX
Add a small desired-state model and persistence layer under .ddx/workers/desired.json, then add a reconcile component around WorkerManager. The reconciler should load desired_count, default worker spec, restart enabled/backoff/max restarts, compare desired server-managed workers to actual server-owned workers, start missing workers, stop excess workers, mark stale running records stopped, and pause restart on dirty-root/operator-attention blockers.

NON-SCOPE
Do not implement process-group child cleanup here beyond using existing Stop. Do not add frontend controls here. The server-managed worker feature is not done until active dogfood queues stop depending on tmux/shell workers and are monitored through the server-managed surface. The operator specifically needs helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq to each have one worker steadily burning down its bead queue, with all in-flight queues expected to burn down in less than 24 hours unless blocked by explicit beads.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
The plan's Migration Plan requires replacing external workers with server-managed desired state. Existing server start/stop at cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql:3271-3285 and worker UI controls at cli/internal/server/frontend/src/routes/nodes/[nodeId]/projects/[projectId]/workers/+layout.svelte:12-42 were one-shot controls, so earlier dogfood used tmux windows and external ddx work processes. After the dependency beads land, the server should be authoritative for routine worker lifecycle.

PROPOSED FIX
Inventory active workers and queues, stop retired tmux/shell workers gracefully, verify no leftover attempt-owned ddx work/claude/codex descendants, set desired_count=1 for helix, ddx, tablespec, pqueue, and heimq via ddx worker/server API, and monitor through server APIs until queues drain or blockers are filed. Update the plan document with migration evidence and any exception beads.

NON-SCOPE

.ddx/beads.jsonl:459:{"acceptance":"1. TestBeadAndWorkPlanUseSameStoreInLinkedWorktree seeds conflicting bead states in a linked worktree and the canonical store, then proves bead show/list/export/ready and work plan report the same lifecycle state for the same bead.
2. TestWorkPlanDoesNotPreferStaleLocalSnapshotWhenCanonicalStoreExists covers the observed ddx-d94f1857 failure mode and proves a closed canonical bead is not returned as next claim from a stale local .ddx snapshot.
3. TestStaleWorktreeMasterDiagnosticReportsMissingExecuteBeadPath seeds worktrees.json with an old .execute-bead-wt master path and proves ddx doctor or an equivalent store diagnostic reports it.
4. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/ddxroot/... ./internal/git/... -run 'TestBeadAndWorkPlanUseSameStoreInLinkedWorktree|TestWorkPlanDoesNotPreferStaleLocalSnapshotWhenCanonicalStoreExists|TestStaleWorktreeMasterDiagnosticReportsMissingExecuteBeadPath' passes.
5. cd cli && go test ./cmd/... passes.
6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.","created_at":"2026-06-13T13:55:11.417260583Z","description":"PROBLEM
In a linked DDx worktree, `ddx bead show/export` reported ddx-d94f1857 as closed while `ddx work plan --json` still ranked the same bead as open and next claim. That can make a worker re-run already-completed work and breaks the queue as source of truth.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/cmd/bead.go:286-320 resolves bead commands through beadWorkspaceRoot(), which calls git.FindNearestDDxWorkspace and can prefer a registered/master worktree. cli/cmd/work_plan.go:80-84 bypasses that path and opens bead.NewStore(resolveBeadStoreRoot(projectRoot)), which prefers the local in-tree .ddx if present. In dogfood on 2026-06-13, the global worktree registry master pointed at an old execute-bead temp path, so bead commands and work plan observed different physical stores.

PROPOSED FIX
- Centralize queue store resolution for all bead/work/status commands behind one project-aware helper.
- Add a linked-worktree regression fixture where the local .ddx snapshot disagrees with the registered/master store and prove bead show, bead list, bead export, bead ready, work plan, and work status use the same store.
- Add a diagnostic when the worktree registry master points at a missing execute-bead path or a path outside the current repository family.
- Ensure the chosen store is visible in verbose/debug output so operators can identify split-brain state.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hand-edit .ddx/beads.jsonl as the fix. Do not remove convention-root support. Do not change bead lifecycle semantics beyond consistent store selection.","id":"ddx-worker-routing-20260613-8f2cf291","issue_type":"bug","labels":["phase:build","area:work","area:beads","kind:bug","kind:reliability","severity:critical","plan:server-managed-workers"],"parent":"ddx-9d1af129","priority":0,"queue-rank":2220,"schema_version":1,"spec-id":"IP-2026-06-13-server-managed-workers","status":"cancelled","title":"work: unify bead store resolution across queue commands","updated_at":"2026-06-13T13:58:36.505448983Z"}
