package server

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"syscall"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func writeFakeProcessScript(t *testing.T, dir, name string) string {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(dir, name)
	pidDir := "\"$DDX_FAKE_PID_DIR\""
	body := fmt.Sprintf(`name=%q
echo $$ > %s/$name.pid
trap '' HUP TERM INT
sleep 300 &
child=$!
echo $child > %s/$name.child.pid
wait $child
`, name, pidDir, pidDir)
	script := "#!/bin/sh\nset -eu\n" + body
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(script), 0o755))
	return path
}

func startFakeProcessGroup(t *testing.T, binPath, pidDir string) (*exec.Cmd, int, int) {
	t.Helper()
	cmd := exec.Command(binPath)
	cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "DDX_FAKE_PID_DIR="+pidDir)
	cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
	go func() {
		_, _ = cmd.Process.Wait()
	}()
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
	})
	pid, childPID := waitForFakeProcessPIDs(t, pidDir, filepath.Base(binPath))
	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
		return processAlive(pid) && processAlive(childPID)
	}, 3*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "fake process tree did not come up")
	return cmd, pid, childPID
}

func waitForFakeProcessPIDs(t *testing.T, pidDir, name string) (int, int) {
	t.Helper()
	pidPath := filepath.Join(pidDir, name+".pid")
	childPath := filepath.Join(pidDir, name+".child.pid")

	deadline := time.Now().Add(3 * time.Second)
	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
		pid, pidErr := readPIDFile(pidPath)
		childPID, childErr := readPIDFile(childPath)
		if pidErr == nil && childErr == nil && pid > 0 && childPID > 0 {
			return pid, childPID
		}
		time.Sleep(25 * time.Millisecond)
	}
	t.Fatalf("timed out waiting for pid files %q and %q", pidPath, childPath)
	return 0, 0
}

func readPIDFile(path string) (int, error) {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)))
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	return pid, nil
}

func waitForProcessExit(t *testing.T, pid int) {
	t.Helper()
	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
		return !processAlive(pid)
	}, 5*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "process %d still alive", pid)
}

func ensureWorkerDir(t *testing.T, m *WorkerManager, id string) string {
	t.Helper()
	dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755))
	return dir
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants proves that Stop can
// terminate fake claude/codex process groups that each spawn sleeping
// descendants when those groups are registered on the worker handle.
func TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	setupBeadStore(t, root)

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	pidDir := filepath.Join(root, "pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pidDir, 0o755))
	claudeBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "claude")
	codexBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "codex")

	claudeCmd, claudePID, claudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, claudeBin, pidDir)
	codexCmd, codexPID, codexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, codexBin, pidDir)
	_ = claudeCmd
	_ = codexCmd

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	beadID := "ddx-stop-managed-tree"
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, beadID)
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-managed-tree")

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, cancelled := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-managed-tree", beadID, now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.childProcessGroups = []int{claudePID, codexPID}
	m.mu.Unlock()

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-managed-tree"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, claudePID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, claudeChildPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, codexPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, codexChildPID)
	require.True(t, cancelled.Load(), "Stop must cancel the in-process worker handle")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent proves repeated Stop calls do not
// duplicate the bead.stopped transition, claim release, or terminal status
// write.
func TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-stop-idem")

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	pidDir := filepath.Join(root, "pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pidDir, 0o755))
	sleepBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "sleepy")
	_, pid, childPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, sleepBin, pidDir)

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-stop-idem")

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-stop-idem", "ddx-stop-idem", now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.record.PID = pid
	handle.record.PGID = pid
	m.mu.Unlock()

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-idem"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, pid)
	waitForProcessExit(t, childPID)

	statusPath := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, "worker-stop-idem", "status.json")
	info1, err := os.Stat(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	events, err := store.EventsByKind("ddx-stop-idem", "bead.stopped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1)

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-idem"))
	time.Sleep(1100 * time.Millisecond)

	info2, err := os.Stat(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, info1.ModTime(), info2.ModTime(), "second Stop must not rewrite terminal status")

	events, err = store.EventsByKind("ddx-stop-idem", "bead.stopped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1, "second Stop must not duplicate bead.stopped")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-stop-idem")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers proves Stop only kills the
// registered managed worker process groups and leaves external reported
// sessions plus unrelated process groups alone.
func TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	setupBeadStore(t, root)

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	managedPIDDir := filepath.Join(root, "managed-pids")
	externalPIDDir := filepath.Join(root, "external-pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(managedPIDDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(externalPIDDir, 0o755))
	claudeBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "claude")
	codexBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "codex")

	managedClaudeCmd, managedClaudePID, managedClaudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, claudeBin, managedPIDDir)
	managedCodexCmd, managedCodexPID, managedCodexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, codexBin, managedPIDDir)
	_ = managedClaudeCmd
	_ = managedCodexCmd

	externalClaudeCmd, externalClaudePID, externalClaudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, claudeBin, externalPIDDir)
	externalCodexCmd, externalCodexPID, externalCodexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, codexBin, externalPIDDir)
	_ = externalClaudeCmd
	_ = externalCodexCmd

	unrelatedCmd := exec.Command("sleep", "300")
	unrelatedCmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
	require.NoError(t, unrelatedCmd.Start())
	go func() {
		_, _ = unrelatedCmd.Process.Wait()
	}()
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = syscall.Kill(-unrelatedCmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
	})

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-stop-skip")

	beadID := "ddx-stop-skip"
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, beadID)
	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-stop-skip", beadID, now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.childProcessGroups = []int{managedClaudePID, managedCodexPID}
	m.mu.Unlock()

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-skip"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedClaudePID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedClaudeChildPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedCodexPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedCodexChildPID)

	require.True(t, processAlive(externalClaudePID), "external Claude session must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalClaudeChildPID), "external Claude descendant must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalCodexPID), "external Codex session must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalCodexChildPID), "external Codex descendant must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(unrelatedCmd.Process.Pid), "unrelated process group must survive Stop")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

}

// TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants proves that Stop can
// terminate fake claude/codex process groups that each spawn sleeping
// descendants when those groups are registered on the worker handle.
func TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	setupBeadStore(t, root)

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	pidDir := filepath.Join(root, "pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pidDir, 0o755))
	claudeBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "claude")
	codexBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "codex")

	_, claudePID, claudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, claudeBin, pidDir)
	_, codexPID, codexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, codexBin, pidDir)

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	beadID := "ddx-stop-managed-tree"
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, beadID)
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-managed-tree")

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, cancelled := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-managed-tree", beadID, now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.childProcessGroups = []int{claudePID, codexPID}
	m.mu.Unlock()

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-managed-tree"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, claudePID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, claudeChildPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, codexPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, codexChildPID)
	require.True(t, cancelled.Load(), "Stop must cancel the in-process worker handle")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent proves repeated Stop calls do not
// duplicate the bead.stopped transition, claim release, or terminal status
// write.
func TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-stop-idem")

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	pidDir := filepath.Join(root, "pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pidDir, 0o755))
	sleepBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "sleepy")
	_, pid, childPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, sleepBin, pidDir)

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-stop-idem")

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-stop-idem", "ddx-stop-idem", now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.record.PID = pid
	handle.record.PGID = pid
	m.mu.Unlock()

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-idem"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, pid)
	waitForProcessExit(t, childPID)

	statusPath := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, "worker-stop-idem", "status.json")
	info1, err := os.Stat(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	events, err := store.EventsByKind("ddx-stop-idem", "bead.stopped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1)

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-idem"))
	time.Sleep(1100 * time.Millisecond)

	info2, err := os.Stat(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, info1.ModTime(), info2.ModTime(), "second Stop must not rewrite terminal status")

	events, err = store.EventsByKind("ddx-stop-idem", "bead.stopped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1, "second Stop must not duplicate bead.stopped")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-stop-idem")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers proves Stop only kills the
// registered managed worker process groups and leaves external reported
// sessions plus unrelated process groups alone.
func TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	setupBeadStore(t, root)

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	managedPIDDir := filepath.Join(root, "managed-pids")
	externalPIDDir := filepath.Join(root, "external-pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(managedPIDDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(externalPIDDir, 0o755))
	claudeBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "claude")
	codexBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "codex")

	_, managedClaudePID, managedClaudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, claudeBin, managedPIDDir)
	_, managedCodexPID, managedCodexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, codexBin, managedPIDDir)

	_, externalClaudePID, externalClaudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, claudeBin, externalPIDDir)
	_, externalCodexPID, externalCodexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, codexBin, externalPIDDir)

	unrelatedCmd := exec.Command("sleep", "300")
	unrelatedCmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
	require.NoError(t, unrelatedCmd.Start())
	go func() {
		_, _ = unrelatedCmd.Process.Wait()
	}()
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = syscall.Kill(-unrelatedCmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
	})

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-stop-skip")

	beadID := "ddx-stop-skip"
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, beadID)
	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-stop-skip", beadID, now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.childProcessGroups = []int{managedClaudePID, managedCodexPID}
	m.mu.Unlock()

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-skip"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedClaudePID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedClaudeChildPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedCodexPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedCodexChildPID)

	require.True(t, processAlive(externalClaudePID), "external Claude session must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalClaudeChildPID), "external Claude descendant must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalCodexPID), "external Codex session must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalCodexChildPID), "external Codex descendant must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(unrelatedCmd.Process.Pid), "unrelated process group must survive Stop")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-aef568e7">     <title>server: stop managed worker process trees idempotently</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Operator Stop must fully clean a server-managed worker, including claude, codex, shell, and sleep descendants, without killing unrelated processes or duplicating bead transitions when Stop is called more than once.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1227 has an idempotence guard for in-memory handles, but the cleanup path still depends on a single recorded PID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 calls terminateProcessGroup only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1288-1289 then cancels the goroutine, which cannot guarantee cleanup of harness descendants when the server-owned worker lacks a process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:22-32 documents that callers must register only processes spawned in their own process group.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce or reuse one cleanup helper that terminates only server-owned managed worker process groups by PGID when available, falling back to registered child process groups for any transitional in-process harness path. Wire WorkerManager.Stop through that helper after the existing bead.stopped event and Unclaim call. The helper must send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, send SIGKILL to remaining descendants, report what it killed, and make repeated Stop calls no-ops for bead transitions and process cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the contract: every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

Too big if any holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs.
- Description names multiple feature-sized pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across ~5+ files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, use a split-first pass.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, children once more; reject third-level splits with an explanation.

If too big, decompose:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child (copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add` only for legitimate child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges; never the parent.
3. Use `parent=<parent-id>` metadata and wire `parent -> child`.
4. Write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path with child IDs, then stop.

Decomposition alone is success. Don't mix it with implementation.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC to concrete evidence (test, file, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Run git/index mutations sequentially; don't parallelize `git add`, `git commit`, or staging/commit commands.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- If `lefthook run pre-commit` depends on staged files, rerun it after staging the exact commit set. A `no-staged-files` run is not acceptance evidence.
- Commit exactly once when green; subject ends with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- Current-bead lifecycle is orchestrator-owned. Do not run `ddx bead update <bead-id> --claim`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --status <status>`, `ddx bead update <bead-id> --unclaim`, or `ddx bead close <bead-id>`. Step 0 allows `ddx bead create`, `ddx bead dep add` for child-to-child or sibling/replacement edges, and `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'`.
- If you cannot finish, write `no_changes_rationale.txt` under the bead metadata `bundle` path before exiting. No commit or rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`.

## no_changes contract

The `no_changes_rationale.txt` file under the bead metadata `bundle` path must contain one of:

- `verification_command: <cmd>` — repo cwd; exit 0 closes, nonzero rejects.
- `status: open` + `reason: <retryable>` — open, smart retry.
- `status: proposed` + `reason: <operator needed>` — operator lane.
- `status: blocked` + `reason: <external blocker>` — blocked lane.

No pseudo-statuses. Bare rationales and `status: needs_investigation` are rejected.

For orchestrator decomposition, add `orchestrator_action: decompose` alongside `status: open`.

## Reports

Reports go under the bead metadata `bundle` path in `.ddx/executions/`. **Never write reports to `/tmp` or outside the repo**. Use a named in-repo path when given; otherwise write `<short-name>.md` there. Commit it with the code.

## Mode and overrides

DDX_MODE=bead_execution: edit code/docs for bead AC. Only broad queue-steward default is overridden; tracker, merge-policy, verification, safety stay active. Bead description/AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and defaults.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other instruction files unless the bead asks.

## Long-running matrix/benchmark beads

For expensive commands (benchmarks, load tests, validation > 60s per variant):

- Write a matrix plan before launching expensive commands: list required configs, output paths, completion criteria.
- Do not re-run the same long-running command unless: (1) the command fingerprint changed (args/env/config), AND (2) you document why prior output is invalid and what changed.
- If a long-running command times out or is incomplete, exit with `status: open` + retryable `reason` in `no_changes_rationale.txt`. Do not silently retry; let the orchestrator decide.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator.   </instructions>   <bead id="ddx-aef568e7">     <title>server: stop managed worker process trees idempotently</title>     <description> PROBLEM
Operator Stop must fully clean a server-managed worker, including claude, codex, shell, and sleep descendants, without killing unrelated processes or duplicating bead transitions when Stop is called more than once.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
cli/internal/server/workers.go:1214-1227 has an idempotence guard for in-memory handles, but the cleanup path still depends on a single recorded PID. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1234 reads WorkerRecord.PID, and cli/internal/server/workers.go:1282-1286 calls terminateProcessGroup only when pid > 0. cli/internal/server/workers.go:1288-1289 then cancels the goroutine, which cannot guarantee cleanup of harness descendants when the server-owned worker lacks a process boundary. cli/internal/server/workers_reap_unix.go:22-32 documents that callers must register only processes spawned in their own process group.

PROPOSED FIX
Introduce or reuse one cleanup helper that terminates only server-owned managed worker process groups by PGID when available, falling back to registered child process groups for any transitional in-process harness path. Wire WorkerManager.Stop through that helper after the existing bead.stopped event and Unclaim call. The helper must send SIGTERM, wait WatchdogKillGrace, send SIGKILL to remaining descendants, report what it killed, and make repeated Stop calls no-ops for bead transitions and process cleanup.

NON-SCOPE
Do not kill external reported workers or interactive Claude/Codex sessions. Do not change bead claim semantics except releasing claims for the stopped/reaped managed worker. Do not implement UI controls.

GOVERNING ARTIFACT AND RELATIONSHIP
Parent bead: ddx-9d1af129. Governing artifact reference: plan:server-managed-workers. No explicit dependency IDs were provided by the parent bead.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants uses fake claude and codex binaries on PATH that spawn sleeping children, calls Stop, and proves none of those descendants remain running.

5. make install is run after the code change lands.

package server

import (
	"context"
	"errors"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"runtime"
	"sync/atomic"
	"syscall"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/ddxroot"
	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/testutils"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

// newIdleHandle returns a manually-constructed workerHandle registered with m
// for tests that drive the watchdog directly without starting a real loop.
func newIdleHandle(t *testing.T, m *WorkerManager, id string, beadID string, startedAt, lastPhaseTS time.Time) (*workerHandle, *atomic.Bool) {
	t.Helper()
	var cancelled atomic.Bool
	cancel := context.CancelFunc(func() { cancelled.Store(true) })
	h := &workerHandle{
		record: WorkerRecord{
			ID:          id,
			Kind:        "work",
			State:       "running",
			Status:      "running",
			ProjectRoot: m.projectRoot,
			StartedAt:   startedAt,
			CurrentBead: beadID,
			CurrentAttempt: &CurrentAttemptInfo{
				AttemptID: id + "-a1",
				BeadID:    beadID,
				Phase:     "running",
				StartedAt: startedAt,
			},
		},
		cancel:       cancel,
		progressDone: make(chan struct{}),
		lastPhaseTS:  lastPhaseTS,
	}
	m.mu.Lock()
	m.workers[id] = h
	m.mu.Unlock()
	return h, &cancelled
}

// seedClaimedBead creates a ready bead and claims it so Unclaim() has work
// to do. Returns the bead store.
func seedClaimedBead(t *testing.T, root string, beadID string) *bead.Store {
	t.Helper()
	ddx := testutils.MakeInitializedDDxRoot(t, root)
	store := bead.NewStore(ddx)
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(context.Background(), &bead.Bead{
		ID:        beadID,
		Title:     "watchdog test bead",
		Status:    bead.StatusOpen,
		IssueType: bead.DefaultType,
	}))
	require.NoError(t, store.Claim(beadID, "worker-test"))
	return store
}

// TestWatchdogSweepReapsStalledWorker is the core AC test: a worker whose
// runtime exceeds WatchdogDeadline AND whose current attempt has sat without
// a phase transition longer than StallDeadline is reaped by one sweep.
func TestWatchdogSweepReapsStalledWorker(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-wd-01")

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	m.WatchdogDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.StallDeadline = 1 * time.Millisecond
	m.WatchdogKillGrace = 10 * time.Millisecond
	defer m.StopWatchdog()

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	// StartedAt is 1s in the past and lastPhaseTS is 1s in the past —
	// both exceed the 1ms deadlines.
	h, cancelled := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-wd-01", "ddx-wd-01",
		now.Add(-1*time.Second), now.Add(-1*time.Second))

	m.watchdogSweep(now)

	m.mu.Lock()
	reaped := h.reaped
	state := h.record.State
	reapReason := h.record.ReapReason
	m.mu.Unlock()

	assert.True(t, reaped, "handle should be flagged reaped")
	assert.Equal(t, "reaped", state, "record.State must flip to 'reaped'")
	assert.Equal(t, "watchdog", reapReason)
	assert.True(t, cancelled.Load(), "watchdog must invoke cancel() for in-process workers")

	// AC: bead claim must be released back to open.
	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-wd-01")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status,
		"bead must return to open after watchdog releases the claim")

	// AC: a bead.reaped event must be on the tracker with reason=watchdog
	// and a duration mentioned in the body.
	events, err := store.EventsByKind("ddx-wd-01", "bead.reaped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1, "expected exactly one bead.reaped event")
	assert.Equal(t, "watchdog", events[0].Summary)
	assert.Contains(t, events[0].Body, "runtime=")
	assert.Contains(t, events[0].Body, "stalled=")
	assert.Contains(t, events[0].Body, "reason=watchdog")
}

data.text: I’m tightening the tests so they match the bead text more literally: the fake binaries will be discovered through `PATH`, and the claim release will be asserted through the bead claim sidecar rather than inferred only from status.
I’ve updated the tests to use `PATH` and to assert claim-lease removal directly. I’m checking the file for any now-unused locals before I rerun the server suite.

//go:build !windows

package server

import (
	"context"
	"fmt"
	"os"
	"os/exec"
	"path/filepath"
	"strconv"
	"strings"
	"syscall"
	"testing"
	"time"

	"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
)

func writeFakeProcessScript(t *testing.T, dir, name string) string {
	t.Helper()
	path := filepath.Join(dir, name)
	pidDir := "\"$DDX_FAKE_PID_DIR\""
	body := fmt.Sprintf(`name=%q
echo $$ > %s/$name.pid
trap '' HUP TERM INT
sleep 300 &
child=$!
echo $child > %s/$name.child.pid
wait $child
`, name, pidDir, pidDir)
	script := "#!/bin/sh\nset -eu\n" + body
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(path, []byte(script), 0o755))
	return path
}

func prependFakeBinToPath(t *testing.T, binDir string) {
	t.Helper()
	t.Setenv("PATH", binDir+string(os.PathListSeparator)+os.Getenv("PATH"))
}

func startFakeProcessGroup(t *testing.T, commandName, pidDir string) (*exec.Cmd, int, int) {
	t.Helper()
	cmd := exec.Command(commandName)
	cmd.Env = append(os.Environ(), "DDX_FAKE_PID_DIR="+pidDir)
	cmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
	require.NoError(t, cmd.Start())
	go func() {
		_, _ = cmd.Process.Wait()
	}()
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = syscall.Kill(-cmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
	})
	pid, childPID := waitForFakeProcessPIDs(t, pidDir, commandName)
	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
		return processAlive(pid) && processAlive(childPID)
	}, 3*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "fake process tree did not come up")
	return cmd, pid, childPID
}

func waitForFakeProcessPIDs(t *testing.T, pidDir, name string) (int, int) {
	t.Helper()
	pidPath := filepath.Join(pidDir, name+".pid")
	childPath := filepath.Join(pidDir, name+".child.pid")

	deadline := time.Now().Add(3 * time.Second)
	for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
		pid, pidErr := readPIDFile(pidPath)
		childPID, childErr := readPIDFile(childPath)
		if pidErr == nil && childErr == nil && pid > 0 && childPID > 0 {
			return pid, childPID
		}
		time.Sleep(25 * time.Millisecond)
	}
	t.Fatalf("timed out waiting for pid files %q and %q", pidPath, childPath)
	return 0, 0
}

func readPIDFile(path string) (int, error) {
	data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	pid, err := strconv.Atoi(strings.TrimSpace(string(data)))
	if err != nil {
		return 0, err
	}
	return pid, nil
}

func waitForProcessExit(t *testing.T, pid int) {
	t.Helper()
	require.Eventually(t, func() bool {
		return !processAlive(pid)
	}, 5*time.Second, 25*time.Millisecond, "process %d still alive", pid)
}

func ensureWorkerDir(t *testing.T, m *WorkerManager, id string) string {
	t.Helper()
	dir := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, id)
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(dir, 0o755))
	return dir
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants proves that Stop can
// terminate fake claude/codex process groups that each spawn sleeping
// descendants when those groups are registered on the worker handle.
func TestManagedWorkerStopKillsClaudeCodexDescendants(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	setupBeadStore(t, root)

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	pidDir := filepath.Join(root, "pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pidDir, 0o755))
	claudeBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "claude")
	codexBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "codex")
	prependFakeBinToPath(t, binDir)

	_, claudePID, claudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "claude", pidDir)
	_, codexPID, codexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "codex", pidDir)

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	beadID := "ddx-stop-managed-tree"
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, beadID)
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-managed-tree")

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, cancelled := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-managed-tree", beadID, now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.childProcessGroups = []int{claudePID, codexPID}
	m.mu.Unlock()
	lease, found, err := store.ClaimLease(beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, found, "expected claim lease before Stop")
	require.Equal(t, "worker-test", lease.Owner)

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-managed-tree"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, claudePID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, claudeChildPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, codexPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, codexChildPID)
	require.True(t, cancelled.Load(), "Stop must cancel the in-process worker handle")
	_, found, err = store.ClaimLease(beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, found, "Stop must release the claim lease")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent proves repeated Stop calls do not
// duplicate the bead.stopped transition, claim release, or terminal status
// write.
func TestManagedWorkerStopIsIdempotent(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, "ddx-stop-idem")

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	pidDir := filepath.Join(root, "pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(pidDir, 0o755))
	sleepBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "sleepy")
	prependFakeBinToPath(t, binDir)
	_, pid, childPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "sleepy", pidDir)

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-stop-idem")

	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-stop-idem", "ddx-stop-idem", now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.record.PID = pid
	handle.record.PGID = pid
	m.mu.Unlock()
	lease, found, err := store.ClaimLease("ddx-stop-idem")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, found, "expected claim lease before Stop")
	require.Equal(t, "worker-test", lease.Owner)

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-idem"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, pid)
	waitForProcessExit(t, childPID)
	_, found, err = store.ClaimLease("ddx-stop-idem")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, found, "first Stop must release the claim lease")

	statusPath := filepath.Join(m.rootDir, "worker-stop-idem", "status.json")
	info1, err := os.Stat(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)

	events, err := store.EventsByKind("ddx-stop-idem", "bead.stopped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1)

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-idem"))
	time.Sleep(1100 * time.Millisecond)

	info2, err := os.Stat(statusPath)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, info1.ModTime(), info2.ModTime(), "second Stop must not rewrite terminal status")
	_, found, err = store.ClaimLease("ddx-stop-idem")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, found, "second Stop must keep the claim lease released")

	events, err = store.EventsByKind("ddx-stop-idem", "bead.stopped")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Len(t, events, 1, "second Stop must not duplicate bead.stopped")

	b, err := store.Get(context.Background(), "ddx-stop-idem")
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, b.Status)
}

// TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers proves Stop only kills the
// registered managed worker process groups and leaves external reported
// sessions plus unrelated process groups alone.
func TestManagedWorkerStopSkipsExternalReportedWorkers(t *testing.T) {
	root := t.TempDir()
	setupBeadStore(t, root)

	binDir := filepath.Join(root, "bin")
	managedPIDDir := filepath.Join(root, "managed-pids")
	externalPIDDir := filepath.Join(root, "external-pids")
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(binDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(managedPIDDir, 0o755))
	require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(externalPIDDir, 0o755))
	claudeBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "claude")
	codexBin := writeFakeProcessScript(t, binDir, "codex")
	prependFakeBinToPath(t, binDir)

	_, managedClaudePID, managedClaudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "claude", managedPIDDir)
	_, managedCodexPID, managedCodexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "codex", managedPIDDir)

	_, externalClaudePID, externalClaudeChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "claude", externalPIDDir)
	_, externalCodexPID, externalCodexChildPID := startFakeProcessGroup(t, "codex", externalPIDDir)

	unrelatedCmd := exec.Command("sleep", "300")
	unrelatedCmd.SysProcAttr = &syscall.SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}
	require.NoError(t, unrelatedCmd.Start())
	go func() {
		_, _ = unrelatedCmd.Process.Wait()
	}()
	t.Cleanup(func() {
		_ = syscall.Kill(-unrelatedCmd.Process.Pid, syscall.SIGKILL)
	})

	m := NewWorkerManager(root)
	defer m.StopWatchdog()
	ensureWorkerDir(t, m, "worker-stop-skip")

	beadID := "ddx-stop-skip"
	store := seedClaimedBead(t, root, beadID)
	now := time.Now().UTC()
	handle, _ := newIdleHandle(t, m, "worker-stop-skip", beadID, now.Add(-time.Second), now.Add(-time.Second))
	m.mu.Lock()
	handle.childProcessGroups = []int{managedClaudePID, managedCodexPID}
	m.mu.Unlock()
	lease, found, err := store.ClaimLease(beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.True(t, found, "expected claim lease before Stop")
	require.Equal(t, "worker-test", lease.Owner)

	require.NoError(t, m.Stop("worker-stop-skip"))
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedClaudePID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedClaudeChildPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedCodexPID)
	waitForProcessExit(t, managedCodexChildPID)
	_, found, err = store.ClaimLease(beadID)
	require.NoError(t, err)
	require.False(t, found, "Stop must release the claim lease")

	require.True(t, processAlive(externalClaudePID), "external Claude session must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalClaudeChildPID), "external Claude descendant must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalCodexPID), "external Codex session must survive Stop")
	require.True(t, processAlive(externalCodexChildPID), "external Codex descendant must survive Stop")
